---
title: "Week XX: Week Title"
subtitle: "EDS 231 — Spring 2026"
date: 
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    highlight: pygments
    css: week-styles.css
    toc: false
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

> Getting oriented: what we'll cover, how we'll work, and why it matters.

---

## Overview

This week we set the foundation for the course. We'll introduce the core concepts, review the syllabus, and get hands-on with the tools we'll use throughout the semester. By the end of this week you should have a clear sense of where we're going and how to succeed.

### Learning Objectives

By the end of this week, you will be able to:

- Describe the course arc and major themes
- Set up your computing environment and verify it works
- Explain the core vocabulary introduced in lecture
- Complete and submit the first assignment

---

### Recommended Reading

📖 Chapter 1 of the course textbook (pp. 1–28) — [Download PDF](#)

---

### Setup

---

## Lecture Materials

### Slides

🖥 Full slide deck from lecture

[View Slides](#){.btn .btn-primary} &nbsp; 

### Code & Data

💻 R scripts and datasets used during the live demo.

[Download .zip →](#)

---

## 📂 Assignments

### Lab X — Lab X Name <span class="badge badge-danger">Graded · 11 pts</span>

**Due: Month DD @ 11:59 pm**

Complete the short data exploration exercise using the provided dataset. Submit a knitted HTML report to the course portal.

[Download Starter File](#){.btn .btn-primary} &nbsp; [Submission Portal](#){.btn .btn-default}

---

## 📚 Additional Resources

| Type | Resource |
|------|----------|
| Article | ["Why Reproducible Research Matters" — Peng (2011), *Science*](#) |
| Video | [R for Data Science — Introduction (YouTube, 22 min)](#) |
| Reference | [RStudio Cheatsheets — Data Wrangling & Visualization](#) |
| Tool | [Course GitHub Repository — starter files and solutions](#) |

---

[← Previous Week](#) &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; [Week 2: Data Structures →](#)
